policy_crossover: MOM median-wage trends intersect with AIScope exposure: 11.2% of occupations sit in the high-wage/high-exposure quadrant while 24.6% combine lower wages with high exposure — a structural tension for SkillsFuture and sector councils as 2026 reasoning-heavy automation compresses premium desk workflows faster than frontline PWM floors adjust.
market_overview: Singapore snapshot (570 SSOC-style rows): mean AI exposure sits near 5.78/10 with a fat right tail in clerical and professional services as 2026 reasoning models absorb cross-document work. PWM-covered frontline roles remain capped but face gradual hardware substitution in pilots; policy focus should pair wage statistics with reskilling velocity, not headline model releases alone.
policy_crossover_zh: MOM工资中位数趋势与AIScope暴露度交叉：11.2%的职业处于高薪且高暴露象限，另有24.6%的职业处于低薪且高暴露区间。随着2026年推理型自动化更快压缩高端文职流程，而一线PWM工资底线调整相对滞后，这对SkillsFuture与行业理事会形成结构性政策张力。
